data.title: Chipotle launches a value-focused family meal offering
data.description:
Chipotle Mexican Grill (NYSE:CMG) announced on Monday a new "Build-Your-Own Chipotle" family meal that will be available for digital order starting on August 26. The company sees the offering as an accessible, value-focused alternative for families, gatherings, and back-to-school routines, emphasizing customization and ease. 

The fast-casual restaurant operator said the family meal is designed to serve four to six people. The digital-exclusive menu option was highlighted as allowing customers to create a customized Chipotle experience at home with family-style portions of main ingredients, offering a choice of protein (including chicken, steak, barbacoa, carnitas, or Sofritas), rice, beans, a premium side such as guacamole or Queso Blanco, three salsas or toppings, lettuce, cheese, two large bags of chips, eight tortillas, and utensils for six. 

The meal is being positioned by Chipotle (NYSE:CMG) as a convenient solution for group occasions requiring no advance planning and can be ready for pickup in as little as 15 minutes. Chipotle (CMG) is promoting the launch with a limited-time $10 discount on the first "Build-Your-Own Chipotle" order through its app or website.

Chipotle (CMG) operates over 3,800 restaurants globally, maintaining a focus on responsibly sourced ingredients and a digitally driven guest experience. 

Shares of Chipotle (CMG) fell 0.2% in premarket trading to $43.55 vs. the 52-week range of $41.18 to $66.74.

data.title: The Home Depot extends tender offer for GMS
data.description:
• The Home Depot (NYSE:HD) said on Monday that its wholly owned subsidiary, Gold Acquisition Sub has extended its tender to acquire all outstanding shares of GMS (GMS) for $110 per share in cash.
  • The offer, initially set to expire on August 22, will now remain open until 11:59 p.m. ET on September 3, 2025.
  • As of the original deadline, about 29.3 million GMS shares or 77% of the total had been tendered.
  • The deal, announced on June 29, 2025, still needs approval from Canadian regulators and requires a majority of shares to be tendered before it can close.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Verint to be acquired by Thoma Bravo for $2B
data.description:
• Verint (NASDAQ:VRNT) said it will be acquired by software investment firm Thoma Bravo in an all-cash deal valuing the company at $2 billion. Shareholders will receive $20.50 per share, an 18% premium to the 10-day average price before reports of a potential sale.
  • Shares of Verint (NASDAQ:VRNT) were up over 12% following the announcement.
  • The transaction, which was unanimously approved by the Verint board of directors, is expected to close before the end of Verint’s current fiscal year.
  • After the deal closes, Verint’s common stock will be delisted from public exchanges. The company said it will suspend quarterly earnings calls, halt quarterly and annual guidance, and discontinue its share repurchase program pending the transaction.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Skyline Builders plans to implement reverse stock split
data.description:
Hong Kong-based civil engineering services provider Skyline Builders (NASDAQ:SKBL) announced on Monday a proposed reverse stock split, subject to shareholder approval.

Shares were +10.59% pre-market to $0.49.

At the upcoming extraordinary general meeting, stockholders will be asked to approve the share consolidation at a ratio of ten ordinary shares into one, or at such lesser whole number as the board may determine in its sole discretion, but not fewer than two shares into one.

The share consolidation is expected to be implemented within one year of stockholders' approval.

data.description:

data.title: Moody’s plans to raise ownership in MERIS Ratings
data.description:
• Moody’s (NYSE:MCO) plans to increase its ownership in MERIS Ratings, a domestic credit rating agency in Egypt.
  • The terms of the transaction were not disclosed.
  • The transaction extends the long-established partnership between the companies and deepens Moody’s presence in the Middle East and Africa. Following the transaction, MERIS will continue to operate as an independent affiliate of Moody’s while developing its own rating methodologies, issuing its own credit ratings, and maintaining a separate management team.

data.title: Chicago Fed National Activity Index ticks down in July
data.description:
July Chicago Fed National Activity Index ("CFNAI"): -0.19 vs. -0.18 in June (revised from -0.10), the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago said on Monday.

The CFNAI's three-month moving average rose to -0.18 from -0.26 in June (revised from -0.22).

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: SLB OneSubsea secures EPC contract for Equinor’s Fram Sør project
data.description:
• Global energy technology company SLB (NYSE:SLB) announced on Monday that its OneSubse joint venture has been awarded an engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) contract by Equinor for a 12-well, all-electric Subsea Production System (SPS) in the Fram Sør field, offshore Norway.
  • SLB said OneSubsea will deliver 4 subsea templates and 12 all-electric subsea trees, eliminating the need for hydraulic fluid supplied by the host platform and keeping topside modifications to a minimum.
  • “Fram Sør is a breakthrough project for our industry — marking the first large-scale all-electric subsea production system,” said Mads Hjelmeland, chief executive officer of SLB OneSubsea. “Not only do all-electric subsea solutions significantly reduce topside needs to make large-scale tiebacks such as the Fram Sør development possible, but they also hold the key to unlock more marginal resources through their reduced footprint and simplified operations.”
  • The project will be developed as a subsea tieback to the host platform Troll C in the North Sea, contributing to the security of energy supply from the Norwegian continental shelf (NCS) to Europe.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Strategy acquires 3,081 bitcoins for ~$357M in past week
data.description:
Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) said on Monday that it acquired 3,081 bitcoins for $356.9M in the week ended August 24, compared to 430 BTC for $51.4M in the week before.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) fell 3.38% to $113,493.21 during the past week. MSTR shares were -4.26% Monday pre-market to $342.89.

During the period of August 18 to August 24, the bitcoin treasury company purchased bitcoins for an average purchase price of $115,829.

Last week's acquisition brings MSTR's total BTC holdings to 632,457. The aggregate purchase price comes to $46.50B and the average purchase price comes to $73,527.

Last week, the company sold 875,301 shares of common stock for net proceeds of $309.9M under its at-the-market program.

Additionally, the company sold 210,100 Strategy Inc 8.00% SERIES A PERPETUAL STRIKE PFD (STRK) preferred shares for net proceeds of $20.4M; 237,336 Strategy Inc 10.00% SER A PERPETUAL STRIFE PFD STK (STRF) preferred shares for $26.6M; and 944 Perpetual Stride Preferred Stock (STRD) shares for $0.1M.

data.title: AEye, Blue-Band form strategic partnership
data.description:
AEye (NASDAQ:LIDR) has entered into a strategic partnership with Blue-Band to accelerate smart city infrastructure with AI-driven lidar solutions.

The partnership enables the deployment of fully integrated, next-generation traffic infrastructure, combining AEye’s Apollo lidar technology with Blue-Band’s Integrator-AI platform as part of the recently unveiled OPTIS initiative.

It will initially focus on deploying integrated solutions for traffic monitoring, incident detection, and infrastructure intelligence, with a shared vision of accelerating the shift toward safer, smarter, and more adaptive cities.

The combined solution leverages AEye’s long-range Apollo lidar, capable of detecting objects up to one kilometer away, with Blue-Band’s AI-powered orchestration platform, bringing together sensing, computing, advanced 3D perception, and AI to deliver real-time insights for transportation and smart infrastructure applications.
